instruction: Using the provided first aid reference text, generate a realistic aftercare handoff note written by a first responder to a caregiver or family member. The note must explain what happened, what was done, what the caregiver should do at home, warning signs to watch for, and when to seek professional help. Ground all instructions primarily in the reference text. You may supplement with widely accepted first aid facts ONLY if the reference text partially covers the topic — but clearly flag the supplementation.
constraints: All home care steps and warning signs must primarily come from the reference text. Do not include procedures, dosages, or medical guidance beyond the seed text unless supplementation is necessary because the reference text only partially covers the topic. Any supplemented information must be explicitly flagged in the output. Language must be calibrated to the caregiver's knowledge level — plain English for non-medical caregivers, more precise for trained ones. Warning signs must be observable without equipment. Do not include clinical measurements (blood pressure, SpO2) unless explicitly in the seed text. If the seed text has no actionable first aid procedure, output skip=true.
